title: Iran’s Downing of Plane and U.S. Rescue Leave Both Sides Dangerously Emboldened
lead: After Iran shot down a U.S. plane and U.S. forces pulled off a risky ground operation to extricate a stranded airman, both sides claimed victory. That confidence could fuel further escalation.

title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
lead: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

title: Trump Says He ‘Exceeded’ His Objectives in Iran. But What Did He Accomplish?
lead: President Trump said that “we have already met and exceeded” his military objectives. But his goals are largely unresolved.

title: 36 Hours of Chaos: The Scramble for a Cease-Fire in Iran
lead: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.

title: Trump Says He Has Agreed to a 2-Week Cease-Fire With Iran
lead: President Trump announced the deal hours after threatening that a “whole civilization will die tonight” if Iran did not allow commercial vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Iran Is Skeptical About Diplomacy for Now, U.S. Intelligence Says
lead: Any decision by Iran to keep fighting would complicate President Trump’s stated goal of trying to end the war within weeks.

title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
lead: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.

title: How Trump Took the U.S. to War With Iran
lead: In a series of Situation Room meetings, President Trump weighed his instincts against the deep concerns of his vice president and a pessimistic intelligence assessment. Here’s the inside story of how he made the fateful decision.

title: Israel Races to Hit Iran Hard While It Still Can, Officials Say
lead: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave the order on Tuesday for the military to accelerate its attacks, with a 48-hour deadline, four people briefed on the matter said.

title: A Toothless Iran? Missile and Drone Strikes Show It Can Still Inflict Pain.
lead: A wave of strikes across the Middle East in recent days shows that Iran has not lost the capacity to retaliate.

title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
lead: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

title: Big Change Seems Certain in Iran. What Kind Is the Question.
lead: Experts say that Iran’s clerical rulers may be too deeply entrenched for Iranians to topple them, and that the U.S. and Israeli strikes risk setting off deeper radicalization or violence.

title: U.S. Searches for Airman After Iran Shoots Down Fighter Jet
lead: One crew member was rescued. The risky operation to find the second airman came as the United States and Israel struck infrastructure targets in Iran, prompting Iranian retaliation in the Gulf.

title: A Cease-Fire for Now in Iran, but a Blow to American Credibility
lead: Critics wonder if this is America’s “Suez moment,” when a leading power signals the start of its international decline.

title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
lead: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.

title: For Iran’s Rulers, Refusing U.S. Demands Is a Risk Worth Taking
lead: The government in Tehran sees capitulating to Washington’s demands on uranium enrichment and ballistic missiles as riskier to its survival than going to war, analysts say.

title: As Attacks Spread, War With Iran Reaches Far Beyond Mideast
lead: NATO downed a missile fired by Iran at Turkey, and a U.S. sub sank an Iranian Navy ship off Sri Lanka, thousands of miles from the war zone.

title: How Israelis Feel About Another Potential War With Iran
lead: The national mood is somewhere between anxiety, resignation and anticipation as President Trump considers whether to attack Iran.

title: Trump’s Next Decision: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel, Whatever the Risk
lead: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.

title: U.S. Military Moves Into Place for Possible Strikes in Iran
lead: President Trump has given no indication that he has made a decision about how to proceed, as diplomatic talks continue.

title: What Are Trump’s Positions on Iran? They Can Change by the Sentence.
lead: The president is no stranger to staking out contradictory stands, part of what his aides say is his negotiating style. But on Iran, his shifting positions are colliding with the consequences of war.

title: For Trump, Military Strike in Iran Could Serve Symbolic Purpose
lead: Some officials in the Trump administration hope an attack would force Iran to give up its nuclear enrichment program. Others have doubts.

title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Vows to ‘Not Back Down’ as Protests Swell
lead: After days of fierce protest, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei accused protesters of trying to “please” President Trump. Iranian authorities signaled further crackdowns on the demonstrations.

title: With Crackdown on Protests, Iran’s Government ‘Is Only Buying Time’
lead: Its security forces have brutally defended the Islamic Republic, but the protests show that many Iranians consider it stagnant and ideologically hollow.

title: As Trump Considers Second Iran Attack, It Could Be Deadlier Than the First
lead: Within days of the U.S. strikes on Iran’s nuclear facilities last year, all sides agreed to a cease-fire. This time could be different.

title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
lead: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: Israel and the U.S. Are Starting to Show Their Different Priorities in Iran
lead: The United States views Iran through a prism of global responsibilities and strategic goals. Israel has a more regional approach. After nearly three weeks of war, their paths are diverging.

title: Standoff With Iran Raises Fresh Doubts About Trump’s Freestyle Diplomacy
lead: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump’s improvisational approach.

title: Trump Says U.S. Is ‘Locked and Loaded’ if Iran Kills Protesters
lead: Mr. Trump’s remarks that he was ready to come to the protesters’ “rescue” are a sharp escalation as protests over economic hardship turned deadly.

title: Ali Larijani, Iran’s De Facto Political Leader, Killed by Israel
lead: The killing, which Iran confirmed, could embolden Iranian hard-liners, as the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

title: Iran Prepared for War but Ready to Negotiate, Foreign Minister Says
lead: The comments came after President Trump said he was “ looking at some very strong options” to curb the Iranian authorities’ harsh repression of anti-government protests.

title: Trump’s Lesson From Risky Rescue: Threaten to Go Harder at Iran
lead: In an expletive-laced social media post, the president said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he would bomb bridges and power plants.

title: Israel Thought It Could Spur Rebellion Inside Iran. That Hasn’t Happened.
lead: President Trump’s hopes that an Israeli plan to ignite an internal uprising against Iran’s theocratic government could bring the war to a swift end have so far been dashed.

title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
lead: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.

title: Trump Says U.S. Is Negotiating End to War, but Iranians Push Back
lead: “Very, very strong talks,” President Trump claimed. Fake news, replied an Iranian parliamentary leader.

title: You Break It, You Own It? Not for Trump When It Comes to Iran.
lead: “We’ll see what happens with the people,” President Trump said as he appeared to be distancing himself from the longer-term consequences of the war in Iran.

title: Fear and Hope for Iranians Trapped Between Bombs and Defiant Rulers
lead: Many in Iran feel helpless in the face of their entrenched system, and some are becoming increasingly embittered by the fierce American and Israeli bombardment.

title: Inside the Fight to Keep Iran Online
lead: Activists spent years preparing for a communications blackout in Iran, smuggling in Starlink satellite internet systems and making digital shutdowns harder for the authorities to enforce.

title: Iran’s Strategy: Expand the War, Increase the Cost, Outlast Trump
lead: The Islamic Republic is aiming to draw out the conflict and broaden the fighting. That would force President Trump to risk more casualties and more political capital.

title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump as Fighting Rages and Missiles Fly
lead: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.

title: Trump Threatens Iran With ‘Massive Armada’ and Presses a Set of Demands
lead: U.S. and European officials say they have put three demands in front of the Iranians, including a permanent end to all enrichment of uranium.

title: A Timeline of the Tensions Between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the U.S.
lead: The governments of both countries have repeatedly cast the other as evil, perpetuating a cycle that has culminated in the present war.

title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
lead: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.

title: ‘Designed to Wreak Havoc’: The Cheap Drones Shaping the War With Iran
lead: Iran has launched waves of Shahed drones to menace Persian Gulf nations. The U.S. has unleashed its own copycat on Iran. It’s a sign of how war is changing.

title: Strikes Deliver Another Blow to Iran’s Domestic Energy Sites
lead: The attacks could worsen the severe electricity shortages that Iranians already face.

title: Trump Signals U.S. Is Prepared for Long War Against Iran
lead: President Trump also declined to rule out sending ground troops into Iran as the conflict spread into the Gulf countries and Lebanon and Israel exchanged strikes.

title: The World Waited on Edge for Trump’s Thinking on Iran. It’s Still Waiting.
lead: Even as the president considers an attack, his State of the Union address offered little more than a brief repetition of vague talking points from recent days.

title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
lead: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

title: Iran Turns to Digital Surveillance Tools to Track Down Protesters
lead: As Iranian authorities restore some online services after crushing antigovernment demonstrations, they are using a technological dragnet to target attendees of the protests.

title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Drones and Vessels to Try to Open Strategic Shipping Lane
lead: The Israeli military said it had killed the spokesman for Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and Iran warned that it could target American and Israeli military personnel.
